content: Just as the 'reflexion' taught in high school English writing refers to reflecting on one’s own text expression to make the narrative more accurate and layered, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this pedagogical framework to ask LLMs to reflect on their own output from the perspective of a language learner refining an essay, rather than just a solver debugging a logical error. This cross-domain framing can reduce overcorrection of logically valid but stylistically inconsistent LLM outputs that plagues many existing self-reflection methods.
